display_name: Vintage B&W TV — P4 (1960s-70s living-room set)

source.notes: B&W televisions of the era used P4 phosphor (bluish-white with cool color point ~565/540 nm component mix). No shadow mask. Aggressive screen curvature characteristic of pre-1980 TVs.

P4 = ZnS:Ag + (Zn,Cd)S:Ag — white (slightly bluish) cathode-ray sulphide, 565/540 nm dual peak, short persistence ~60 µs (corrected from 0.5 ms — that was an order-of-magnitude error). Standard NTSC B&W TV phosphor 1946-1980s. Chromaticity xy (0.270, 0.300) per Wikipedia phosphor table. Modern Cd-free P4 variants shift xy slightly. Also see 7JP4 article.
